reasoning:
To resolve positively, Eswatini needs a large spike in fatalities, more than ten times the (1 + 30-day average of the past 360 days). Based on the 1.750 reference value, Eswatini has experienced roughly 9 ACLED-recorded fatalities over the last 360 days. The resolution threshold is >17.5 fatalities (so 18 or more) within the 30 days preceding each resolution date.

There are currently zero credible reports or indications of major violence, protests, or institutional unrest in Eswatini as of late March 2025. Given that Eswatini is a very small country with limited political violence outside of the exceptional 2021 protests, the baseline likelihood of seeing 18+ political fatalities in a given 30-day window is historically very low, estimated below 2%. 

For the nearest date (April 6), 23 of the 30 days have already elapsed with no reports of mass fatalities, practically flooring the probability. I apply the systemic probability floor (0.05) to the short horizons due to this lack of evidence. As the horizon extends to September 2025, uncertainty marginally increases as the potential for unforeseen socio-political triggers (such as those observed in 2021) goes slightly up, adjusting distant probabilities upward to 0.08, but remaining highly doubtful overall.
